seoTitle: Sort a Confluence Table: 2026 Quick Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to sort a Confluence table in 2026, from one-click header sorting on published pages to multi-level rules inside Confluence Databases.
intro: Reading through an unsorted table slows down every report review. This guide shows how to sort data inside Confluence tables in 2026, from simple header clicks to layered multi-column rules in a database object.

steps.heading: Sort a standard table
steps.body: Hover over a published table header and click the native sorting arrows icon.

steps.heading: Toggle sort direction
steps.body: Click again to toggle between ascending (A-Z) and descending (Z-A) order.

steps.heading: Open a Confluence Database
steps.body: For more control, open an active Confluence Database object and click the Sort button on the toolbar.

steps.heading: Add sort rules
steps.body: Click Add Sort Rule, select your primary value column, and define your ordering conditions.

steps.heading: Layer a secondary rule
steps.body: Add a secondary sort constraint, such as ordering by Status before Due Date, to organize complex projects.

steps.heading: Lock and publish
steps.body: Click Publish to lock the saved default sort order for your workspace.

faq.question: Can I sort a Confluence table without editing the page?
faq.answer: Yes, hovering over a published table header and clicking the sort arrows works directly on the live view.

faq.question: Can I sort by more than one column at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Confluence Databases support layering multiple sort rules, such as sorting by Status first and then Due Date.

faq.question: Will sorting a table mess up my formatting?
faq.answer: No, the data viewing canvas rearranges rows without corrupting the underlying cell formatting.

faq.question: Can viewers change the sort order without affecting others?
faq.answer: Yes, viewer sort override controls allow team members to toggle the display order dynamically without changing the saved default.
